_doc: Embedded tactical visuals for The Client Recovery SOP For Agencies. {palette} resolves at runtime.

embedded-1.notes: Part 2 lead visual. The 10-stage recovery flowchart, in order. Used as the SOP at a glance.
embedded-1.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial flowchart graphic showing the 10 stages of client recovery, in order. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. No font names, no typography labels, no instructions inside the image.
3. Spell every word and number exactly as written.

COMPOSITION: 10 stage cards arranged in two horizontal rows of 5 cards each, connected by thin cyan arrow lines flowing left to right within each row, and a thin descending arrow connecting the end of row 1 to the start of row 2. Each card: large stage number, stage name on a single line, one-line summary below.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 10 Stages of Client Recovery"
SUBTITLE: "Sequential. Skim some. Skip none. The order is the work."

ROW 1 (left to right):
Stage 01 "Empathetic Acknowledgment": Hear the emotion before you respond to the substance
Stage 02 "Transparent Ownership": Name what is on you, in plain words, no soft-own
Stage 03 "Balanced Error Presentation": Size the mistake honestly, not bigger or smaller
Stage 04 "Detailed Explanation": Walk the chronology, no justifications
Stage 05 "Damage Assessment": Quantify the impact in the metrics that matter

ROW 2 (left to right):
Stage 06 "Encourage Collaboration": Ask what good looks like before you propose the plan
Stage 07 "Actionable Steps": Owner, date, outcome for every step
Stage 08 "Continuous Communication": Set the cadence and channel in writing
Stage 09 "Positive Conclusion": Forward-facing close, no corporate sign-off
Stage 10 "Regular Follow-Up": Hold the cadence through 30, 60, and 90 days

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Stage numbers: large condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4 (e.g., 01, 02)
- Stage names: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Summary lines: small medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF at 85% opacity
- Connector arrows: thin lines in bright cyan #06B6D4

BACKGROUND: deep indigo #1A0B4B to electric purple #7C3AED diagonal gradient.
CARD BACKGROUNDS: rounded rectangles with thin bright cyan outlines on translucent dark indigo fills.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no chat bubble icons, no phone icons.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference flowchart.

embedded-2.notes: Part 1 and Part 4 visual. The 3-tier severity routing matrix in table format.
embedded-2.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference table showing the 3-tier severity routing matrix for client recovery. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No platform brand logos.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The Severity Routing Matrix"
SUBTITLE: "Three tiers. Channel, response window, follow-up, cadence. Stop guessing under pressure."

A table with 6 columns and 3 data rows plus a header row.

HEADER ROW (cyan pills): "TIER", "WHAT IT IS", "CHANNEL", "RESPONSE WINDOW", "WRITTEN FOLLOW-UP", "CHECK-IN CADENCE"

ROW 1 (Tier 1 row):
Cell 1: "Tier 1"
Cell 2: "Minor frustration. Late report, typo, missed Slack. Relationship fine."
Cell 3: "Slack or email"
Cell 4: "Within 4 hours"
Cell 5: "None beyond initial reply"
Cell 6: "One follow-up at 7 days"

ROW 2 (Tier 2 row):
Cell 1: "Tier 2"
Cell 2: "Actively pissed. Blown deadline, deliverable miss, surprise bill."
Cell 3: "Phone or Loom plus written"
Cell 4: "Within 24 hours"
Cell 5: "Plan with owners and dates within 48 hours"
Cell 6: "Weekly written for 30 days"

ROW 3 (Tier 3 row):
Cell 1: "Tier 3"
Cell 2: "Account at risk. Real consequence to client business."
Cell 3: "Video or in-person plus written"
Cell 4: "Within 24 hours"
Cell 5: "Full Recovery Plan within 5 business days"
Cell 6: "Weekly for 30 days, then biweekly to 90"

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Header row pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Tier column: bold sans-serif, off-white
- Data cells: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF in alternating slightly darker indigo backgrounds
- Tier 3 row: subtle bright cyan accent border to indicate higher severity

BACKGROUND: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no platform brand logos.
- No emoji, no clip art, no warning triangle icons.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference table.

embedded-3.notes: Part 2 callout visual. Before-and-after rewrite card showing how to replace the word but in recovery messages.
embedded-3.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial reference card showing before-and-after rewrites of recovery messages, with the word but removed. Aspect 16:9.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word exactly as written. Render all punctuation as shown.
3. No font names or typography labels.

COMPOSITION: a two-column layout with 3 paired rows. Left column header reads "BEFORE" in red, right column header reads "AFTER" in cyan. Each pair shows a recovery line with the word but, then the rewritten version without it.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 'No But' Rule"
SUBTITLE: "The word 'but' cancels every acknowledgment that came before it. Replace with 'and', a period, or nothing."

LEFT COLUMN HEADER PILL: "BEFORE" (rendered in red text, dark indigo pill background)
RIGHT COLUMN HEADER PILL: "AFTER" (rendered in bright cyan text, dark indigo pill background)

ROW 1 BEFORE: "I understand you're frustrated, but the brief said the launch was Friday."
ROW 1 AFTER: "I understand you're frustrated. The brief said Friday, and I should have flagged the mismatch earlier."

ROW 2 BEFORE: "We did our best, but we missed the date."
ROW 2 AFTER: "We missed the date. Here is what happened and what I am doing about it."

ROW 3 BEFORE: "Your team approved this, but the result was below the bar."
ROW 3 AFTER: "Your team approved this. I should have surfaced the risk before we shipped."

BOTTOM CALLOUT BAR: "Same rule applies to 'however', 'although', and 'that said'. Any word that signals 'I am about to undo what I just acknowledged'."

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- BEFORE pill: condensed mono-spaced, bright red #EF4444 on dark indigo pill, uppercase
- AFTER pill: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4 on dark indigo pill, uppercase
- Before lines: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF at 70% opacity, with the word but rendered in bright red #EF4444
- After lines: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF at 100% opacity
- Bottom callout: small italic medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF inside cyan-outlined panel

BACKGROUND: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient.
ROW BACKGROUNDS: rounded rectangles with thin outlines, before in faded red, after in bright cyan.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no checkmark or X icons.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K reference card.

embedded-4.notes: Part 7 visual. Lookup table of 12 mistakes paired with the corrective move.
embedded-4.prompt:
Do not render any of these instructions as text in the image. Render only the labeled content described below.

Editorial lookup table showing 12 mistakes operators make in client recovery, paired with the corrective move. Aspect 5:4.

CRITICAL TEXT-RENDERING RULES:
1. The image must contain ONLY the text pieces listed below.
2. Spell every word and number exactly as written.
3. No font names or typography labels.

TOP CENTER TITLE (large): "The 12 Mistakes To Never Make"
SUBTITLE: "Each one named. Each one paired with the corrective move."

A two-column table with 12 numbered data rows plus a header row.

HEADER ROW (cyan pills): "THE MISTAKE", "THE CORRECTIVE MOVE"

ROW 1: Mistake "01 Using 'but'" | Move "Replace with 'and', a period, or nothing"
ROW 2: Mistake "02 Going silent" | Move "Three-sentence acknowledgment within 24 hours"
ROW 3: Mistake "03 Defensive language" | Move "Delete every defensive sentence on first pass"
ROW 4: Mistake "04 Blame-shifting" | Move "Own at the firm level, address cause internally"
ROW 5: Mistake "05 Over-discounting before listening" | Move "Never offer credit before client is heard"
ROW 6: Mistake "06 Promising what you cannot deliver" | Move "Promise specific reductions, not zero"
ROW 7: Mistake "07 Fixing-by-email when a call is needed" | Move "Request call within 24 hours, written before and after"
ROW 8: Mistake "08 Calling when an email is better" | Move "Tier 1 stays written, calls only for high-emotion"
ROW 9: Mistake "09 Over-explaining" | Move "Trim 30% on every recovery message before sending"
ROW 10: Mistake "10 Dragging in your team" | Move "One sender, one recipient, team informed not cc'd"
ROW 11: Mistake "11 Throwing your team under the bus" | Move "Team identity stays internal, always"
ROW 12: Mistake "12 Surprise discounts as guilt offerings" | Move "Ask before crediting: 'would this feel like the right close?'"

TYPOGRAPHY:
- Top title: bold sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF
- Subtitle: medium sans-serif, slate #1E293B inside off-white pill panel
- Header row pills: condensed mono-spaced, bright cyan #06B6D4, uppercase
- Mistake column: bold sans-serif, off-white, with row number in bright cyan condensed mono-spaced
- Corrective move column: medium sans-serif, off-white #FAF9FF in alternating slightly darker indigo backgrounds

BACKGROUND: deep indigo to electric purple diagonal gradient.
ROW BACKGROUNDS: alternating intensity rounded rectangles with thin bright cyan outlines.

WHAT TO AVOID:
- No people, no faces, no checkmark or X icons.
- No emoji, no clip art.
- No text beyond what is listed.

Color palette: {palette}.

Output: a single high-quality 2K lookup table.
